title: The Year of the Death of Ricardo Reis
author: José Saramago

setting.note: In 1936 Ricardo Reis, one of Fernando Pessoa's poetic alter egos, returns from Brazil to Lisbon just after the poet's death, and drifts through the rainy city, visited by Pessoa's ghost, as fascism rises across Europe and Salazar's shadow deepens at home. Saramago maps a real Lisbon: the hotel where Reis lodges, the cemetery where he speaks with the dead Pessoa, the viewpoint over the Tagus, the river and its docks, and the watchful capital of the New State.

palette.note: The grey-blue of the rainy Tagus and the poet's settled melancholy, the weather of a city and a man waiting for a storm. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
